messages.content:
Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn build-request-map
  "Create the request map from the HttpServletRequest object."
  [^HttpServletRequest request])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn build-request-map
  "Create the request map from the HttpServletRequest object."
  [^HttpServletRequest request]
  {:server-port        (.getServerPort request)
   :server-name        (.getServerName request)
   :remote-addr        (.getRemoteAddr request)
   :uri                (.getRequestURI request)
   :query-string       (.getQueryString request)
   :scheme             (keyword (.getScheme request))
   :request-method     (keyword (.toLowerCase (.getMethod request) Locale/ENGLISH))
   :protocol           (.getProtocol request)
   :headers            (get-headers request)
   :content-type       (.getContentType request)
   :content-length     (get-content-length request)
   :character-encoding (.getCharacterEncoding request)
   :ssl-client-cert    (get-client-cert request)
   :body               (.getInputStream request)})

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn merge-servlet-keys
  "Associate servlet-specific keys with the request map for use with legacy
  systems."
  [request-map
   ^HttpServlet servlet
   ^HttpServletRequest request
   ^HttpServletResponse response])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn merge-servlet-keys
  "Associate servlet-specific keys with the request map for use with legacy
  systems."
  [request-map
   ^HttpServlet servlet
   ^HttpServletRequest request
   ^HttpServletResponse response]
  (merge request-map
         {:servlet              servlet
          :servlet-request      request
          :servlet-response     response
          :servlet-context      (.getServletContext servlet)
          :servlet-context-path (.getContextPath request)}))

(defn open?
  "Returns true if the Socket is open, false otherwise."
  [socket])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= [[:open? true] [:open? false] [:close]]

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn open?
  "Returns true if the Socket is open, false otherwise."
  [socket]
  (boolean (p/-open? socket)))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn upgrade-request?
  "Returns true if the request map is a websocket upgrade request."
  [request])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn upgrade-request?
  "Returns true if the request map is a websocket upgrade request."
  [request]
  (let [{{:strs [connection upgrade]} :headers} request]
    (and upgrade
         connection
         (re-find #"\b(?i)upgrade\b" connection)
         (.equalsIgnoreCase "websocket" upgrade))))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn websocket-response?
  "Returns true if the response contains a websocket listener."
  [response])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn websocket-response?
  "Returns true if the response contains a websocket listener."
  [response]
  (contains? response ::listener))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn request-protocols
  "Returns a collection of websocket subprotocols from a request map."
  [request])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn request-protocols
  "Returns a collection of websocket subprotocols from a request map."
  [request]
  (some-> (:headers request)
          (get "sec-websocket-protocol")
          (str/split #"\s*,\s*")))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn wrap-flash
  "If a :flash key is set on the response by the handler, a :flash key with
  the same value will be set on the next request that shares the same session.
  This is useful for small messages that persist across redirects."
  [handler])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: ring-core/test/ring/middleware/test/flash.clj

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn wrap-flash
  "If a :flash key is set on the response by the handler, a :flash key with
  the same value will be set on the next request that shares the same session.
  This is useful for small messages that persist across redirects."
  [handler]
  (fn
    ([request]
     (let [request (flash-request request)]
       (-> (handler request) (flash-response request))))
    ([request respond raise]
     (let [request (flash-request request)]
       (handler request
                (fn [response] (respond (flash-response response request)))
                raise)))))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn response
  "Returns a skeletal Ring response with the given body, status of 200, and no
  headers."
  [body])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (@response :status) 201))))
- (is (= (@response :status) 200))
- (is (nil? (:body @response)))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn response
  "Returns a skeletal Ring response with the given body, status of 200, and no
  headers."
  [body]
  {:status  200
   :headers {}
   :body    body})

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn header
  "Returns an updated Ring response with the specified header added."
  [resp name value])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (get-in @response [:headers "X-Server"]) "Bar"))
- (is (= (get-in @response [:headers "Content-Type"]) "text/plain"))
- (is (= @response {:headers {"Content-Type" "image/png"}}))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn header
  "Returns an updated Ring response with the specified header added."
  [resp name value]
  (assoc-in resp [:headers name] (str value)))

(defn content-type
  "Returns an updated Ring response with the a Content-Type header corresponding
  to the given content-type."
  [resp content-type])

Observed behavior in repo tests:
- (is (= (@response :content-type) "text/plain"))
- (is (= (@response :content-type) "text/plain"))
- (is (fn? content-type-response)))

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn content-type
  "Returns an updated Ring response with the a Content-Type header corresponding
  to the given content-type."
  [resp content-type]
  (header resp "Content-Type" content-type))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn byte-array-store
  "Returns a function that stores multipart file parameters as an array of
  bytes. The multipart parameters will be stored as maps with the following
  keys:

  :filename     - the name of the uploaded file
  :content-type - the content type of the uploaded file
  :bytes        - an array of bytes containing the uploaded content"
  [])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: ring-core/test/ring/middleware/test/multipart_params.clj
- related test file: ring-core/test/ring/middleware/multipart_params/test/byte_array.clj

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn byte-array-store
  "Returns a function that stores multipart file parameters as an array of
  bytes. The multipart parameters will be stored as maps with the following
  keys:

  :filename     - the name of the uploaded file
  :content-type - the content type of the uploaded file
  :bytes        - an array of bytes containing the uploaded content"
  []
  (fn [item]
    (-> (select-keys item [:filename :content-type])
        (assoc :bytes (IOUtils/toByteArray ^InputStream (:stream item))))))

(defn merge-servlet-keys
  "Associate servlet-specific keys with the request map for use with legacy
  systems."

   ^HttpServlet servlet
   ^HttpServletRequest request

          :servlet-response     response
          :servlet-context      (.getServletContext servlet)

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn wrap-stacktrace-web
  "Wrap a handler such that exceptions are caught and a response containing
  a HTML representation of the exception and stacktrace is returned."
  [handler])

Contract type: docstring_contract
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn wrap-stacktrace-web
  "Wrap a handler such that exceptions are caught and a response containing
  a HTML representation of the exception and stacktrace is returned."
  [handler]
  (fn
    ([request]
     (try
       (handler request)
       (catch Throwable ex
         (ex-response request ex))))
    ([request respond _]
     (try
       (handler request respond (fn [ex] (respond (ex-response request ex))))
       (catch Throwable ex
         (respond (ex-response request ex)))))))

Implement the following Clojure function from a real library.

(defn wrap-lint
  "Wrap a handler to validate incoming requests and outgoing responses
  according to the current Ring specification. An exception is raised if either
  the request or response is invalid."
  [handler])

Observed in repo tests:
- related test file: ring-devel/test/ring/middleware/test/lint.clj

Contract type: docstring_and_test
Provide only the function implementation.

(defn wrap-lint
  "Wrap a handler to validate incoming requests and outgoing responses
  according to the current Ring specification. An exception is raised if either
  the request or response is invalid."
  [handler]
  (fn
    ([request]
     (check-req request)
     (let [response (handler request)]
       (check-resp response)
       response))
    ([request respond raise]
     (check-req request)
     (handler request
              (fn [response]
                (check-resp response)
                (respond response))
              raise))))
